title: Remove old claude documentation
notes:
There are a lot of stray readme files, excessive prose in .lean files, and claudy stuff (Memory, Orient, etc). Some of their content may be useful. Some of it may be quite innacurate now. You'll need to review what around, determine what's worth keeping, and integrating it into the beads workflow in the right way. Please also describe what the right, beads way of doing that kind of documentation is
Survey done. Measured: 36,844 lines Lean (124 files) vs ~4,200 lines of prose about the project. 9,216 comment lines = 29.4% of non-blank Lean.

FIXED THIS SESSION (all verified, lake build green, 1333 jobs):
- README.md advertised a REFUTED theorem ('So Ch(K) and V are homotopy equivalent!') that LabelSpace.lean:237 says is false for branching K. Rewrote; added the braid result.
- CLAUDE.md contradicted itself 30 lines apart (:14 keep MEMORY.md as the status board / :44 do NOT use MEMORY.md files).
- Schedule/DESIGN.md asserted refuted lemma L7 at :289 having refuted it at :262.
- Salvetti/BRAID.md documents a construction that does not exist (no ⋊, no K^⊗n, no BrMonCat anywhere). SUPERSEDED banner added; Cubical-w7x does the real work.
- 9 .lean docstrings cited ClaudeSetup.md / STRUCTURE.md — files that do not exist.
- Tracked files (incl. Chains/Segal.lean:817) linked into ~/.claude/.../memory/ with [[wikilinks]] — a private, non-versioned dir. Any collaborator cloning this repo gets dangling pointers.

Durable facts migrated to bd remember: no-global-chart, refine-bridge-carries-hypotheses, no-swap-braiding-is-created, lean-gotchas, refuted-claims.

Remaining work = epic Cubical-ndf (10 beads).
close_reason:
DONE. lake build CubeChains green (2010 jobs), zero sorry outside quarantine.

MEASURED: 36,844 lines of Lean vs ~4,200 lines of prose ABOUT the project. 9,216 comment lines = 29.4% of non-blank Lean.

THE FOUR DOCS THAT WERE ACTIVELY WRONG (not merely stale):
1. README.md advertised a REFUTED theorem — 'So Ch(K) and V are homotopy equivalent!' — while LabelSpace.lean:237 says that step 'fails for branching K' and Testing/TwoSquares.lean is the machine-checked counterexample. (User has since rewritten the README themselves.)
2. CLAUDE.md contradicted itself 30 lines apart: :14 'MEMORY.md is the live status board, keep it updated' vs :44 'do NOT use MEMORY.md files'.
3. Schedule/DESIGN.md asserted lemma L7 at :289 having REFUTED it at :262.
4. Salvetti/BRAID.md's headline object G K := ⨆ ConcGrpd(K^⊗n) ⋊ Sₙ does not exist in the code (no ⋊, no K^⊗n, no BrMonCat anywhere). Superseded by BraidFunctor.lean's ConcCatN, which grades by event count in ONE K. SUPERSEDED banner added; the rebase-or-retire call is Cubical-w7x, left open because it is a MATH decision tied to the paper.
Plus: a memory auto-loaded every session claimed the repo was sorry-free 'except Research/Conjectures.lean' — that folder does not exist and there are zero sorries.

STRUCTURAL FIX: tracked files pointed into ~/.claude/projects/.../memory/, a machine-local dir that is NOT in git — including a LEAN SOURCE FILE (Chains/Segal.lean:817, 'See [[cubechains-segal]]'). Anyone cloning the repo got dangling pointers into one laptop. All removed.

DIAGNOSIS of why this happened (the real deliverable): the junk prose is DEFENSIVE, not explanatory — 'sorry-free' x10, 'unconditional' x7 in one file, 'assumption-free' x7 in another. All true, all worthless to a reader, all addressed to the requester. It is a status report to a grader, misfiled into a source file. Prior instruction attempts failed because they were PROHIBITIONS WITHOUT DESTINATIONS: 'don't write process prose' lost to 'leave the next agent enough context', which had a concrete affordance (a markdown file) while the prohibition had none. Beads supplies the missing destination.

THE RULE, now in CLAUDE.md: if a fact would become false when a bead closes, it belongs in the bead, not in a file. Plus three MECHANICAL tests (would it become false on close? does it have a tense? does it read as an argument that your work is good?), budgets not adjectives (<=1 line of intent per declaration, <=10 per module docstring), and a destinations table. Key correction discovered mid-work: bd remember is NOT git-portable (issues.jsonl exports only _type:issue; the Dolt DB is gitignored; a clone fetches refs/heads/* only) — so durable knowledge that must survive a clone goes in a PINNED BEAD or in git, and CLAUDE.md stays the constitution because it is the only thing that loads when bd is absent.

Remaining work is tracked under Cubical-ndf. Open: w7x (BRAID.md, needs the user's math call).

title: Audit the true import cone of the braid result
description:
Timeboxed. Compute what hasGlobalEventNaming_iff_braidPure (Salvetti/PurityHDA.lean) actually transitively imports. The §8.1 claim (Foundations + Chains + Arrangements + Salvetti + 3 Schedule files) is a BELIEF, not a measurement. Verify it before moving any file.

Watch for landmine #1: an import of equivWedgeCat/refineToWedge anywhere in the cone means a side condition (NonSelfLinked / AdmitsAltitude) is silently present and the 'unconditional' claim is at risk. Check with #print axioms on the headline theorem.

DONE WHEN: a written file list (the cone), plus a diff of it against the §8.1 claim, plus #print axioms output for hasGlobalEventNaming_iff_braidPure.

title: DECISION: enrich over Cat, or over the braid category?
description:
USER DECIDES. Do not close this from an agent session.

HARD CONSTRAINT (§4): you CANNOT literally enrich over the braid category as hom-objects. Enrichment over V needs hom-OBJECTS in V. The hom-objects here must be groupoids — their objects are the 1-cells. The braid category's objects are strand counts. So hom-objects cannot live in Braid.

What IS true, and is the flashy statement: CFund K is a Grpd-enriched category equipped with a MONOIDAL 2-FUNCTOR to the delooped braid category, under which 1-cell composition maps to the braid TENSOR (concatenating executions adds strand counts). Both halves already exist: Flow/Flow.lean (flowHom, flowComp) and Salvetti/BraidDeloop.lean (braidDeloopComp, where composition really is n (x) m = n + m).

OPTION A — enrich in Cat (the doc's recommendation). mathlib has NO monoidal structure on Grpd (Cat has CartesianMonoidalCategory; Grpd has only Grpd.has_pi), so EnrichedCategory Grpd is not available off the shelf. Cat hom-objects Cat.of (ConcCat (K;u,v)), composition via FreeGroupoid.lift whose universal property IS strict (lift_spec is an equality). EnrichedCategory Cat = a genuine strict 2-category. Only missing input: chambersConcat associativity/unitality.

OPTION B — build the monoidal structure on Grpd first. More faithful to the intended statement, strictly more work, and mathlib will not help.

TRAP: strictness cannot pass through freeGroupoidProdEquiv — it is Localization.uniq, characterised only up to natural iso. This is what kills the naive Grpd route.

The user's §10 note asks whether we can enrich over the braid category directly (objects = naturals, morphisms n->n = braid words). Per §4 that is exactly the thing that does not typecheck as an enrichment; it IS the monoidal 2-functor. Confirm the user wants the 2-functor framing.

title: Single lake target for the braid thread: green, sorry-free, axiom-clean
description:
The acceptance gate for the whole isolation epic, and the regression check every later ticket runs.

DONE WHEN: `lake build <braid target>` is green; no sorry/admit in the cone; #print axioms on hasGlobalEventNaming_iff_braidPure yields exactly propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound.

title: Separate the braid thread from the rest of the repo
description:
BRAID_ENRICHMENT_CLEANUP.md §8.1. The braid result depends on: Foundations/, Chains/, Arrangements/, Salvetti/, plus exactly three files from Schedule/ (EventNaming.lean, EventMapBij.lean, Orientation.lean). Everything else in Schedule/ (schedule space, atlas, cones, charts, MORSE.md), all of Cylinder/, and all of Testing/ is NOT on the critical path.

DONE WHEN: a single lake target builds the whole braid result and nothing else, green + sorry-free + axiom-clean.

title: READ FIRST: braid-thread landmines and refuted claims
description:
Pinned reference. Source: BRAID_ENRICHMENT_CLEANUP.md §6, §7, §8.3, §10.

LANDMINES (each cost real time):
1. equivWedgeCat / refineToWedge CARRY NonSelfLinked + AdmitsAltitude. Routing through them silently imports both while the statement LOOKS unconditional. Never route through the RefineObj<->Ch bridge when the point is to have no side conditions. RefineConcat.append is unusable for the same reason. Unconditional replacements: Chains/Segal.lean's chConcat / wedgeInclL / wedgeInclR.
2. (n :: A) ++ B vs n :: (A ++ B) are defeq but not syntactically equal, and sit inside serialWedge/pushout TYPE arguments. rw/simp/reassoc/Category.comp_id silently fail to match. Use erw with FULLY EXPLICIT arguments (bare erw triggers a whnf blow-up through pushout/colimit).
3. Type's morphisms are bundled TypeCat.Hom; comp_apply/types_comp_apply do NOT fire in simp (ConcreteCategory outParams wreck the discrimination keys). Use exact/defeq.
4. Functor.map_id / map_comp unqualified resolve to the MONAD Functor under `open CategoryTheory`. Write CategoryTheory.Functor.map_id.

REFUTED - DO NOT RESURRECT:
1. "evPerm f = 1 for every morphism <-> K is an HDA" is FALSE, machine-checked (forall_isRun_of_evPerm_one). It forces every chain to be a run, i.e. K has NO concurrency. Witness: the square has a global naming AND a non-run chain. PURITY IS A STATEMENT ABOUT LOOPS.
2. Lifting a chain loop does NOT give an execution loop. A lifted loop at `a` is a path (a, L0) ~> (a, M): same chain, DIFFERENT line. The line must be SHIFTED back (exists_lineShift). Lines must be shifted, not just lifted.

DO NOT ATTEMPT:
- Do not shorten braidFunctor. It is ~70 lines and its proof is already the right one.
- Do not reuse salSumEquiv for the braid tensor. COM.directSum's ground set is only the WITHIN-block pairs; the extension-by-block-order is the whole content.
- Do not hand-roll freeGroupoidProdEquiv. It comes free from mathlib's Localization/Prod.lean.

title: DECISION: generalize Ch/Lines/ConcCat to take endpoints, or keep repoint as the idiom?
description:
USER DECIDES. §8.2 says 'Decide once.' — so decide once.

OPTION A: generalize Ch / Lines / ConcCat to take endpoints as parameters.
OPTION B: keep BPSet.repoint K u v (Flow/ChainConcat.lean) as the official idiom and delete the bipointing from everything that does not use it.

The evidence for B: K.repoint K.init K.final = K is rfl (structure eta). That is a strong hint that repoint is the cheap, honest move and A is churn.

DONE WHEN: the choice is recorded here, with the reason.

title: The enrichment: build Fund, CFund, and the projection
description:
BRAID_ENRICHMENT_CLEANUP.md §3, §4. Three-tier picture, each the quotient of the last:
  1-cells = (chain, line)  2-cells = braids         ConcGrpd K   <- CFund
  1-cells = chains         2-cells = dihomotopies   Pi_1(P K)    <- Fund
  1-cells = trace classes  2-cells = trivial        pi_1(K)      <- classical

CFund -> Fund is literally 'forget the line', and it is a projection. Fullness is ALREADY PROVED (linesRestrict_surjective + exists_conc_zigzag, Salvetti/PurityHDA.lean). Kernel = the local pure braid groups.

title: ARCHITECTURE.md is stale by omission — it is the map and it is missing the headline
description:
The map file does not mention: (a) the ENTIRE `Flow/` folder (Flow.lean, ChainConcat.lean — both in the root build); (b) the braid-enrichment result, i.e. the thing we think is publishable; (c) ~30 files across Salvetti/ (covered in 3 lines naming 2 of 22 files), Schedule/, Chains/, Arrangements/, Cobordisms/.

Also move the 'Open questions — cylinder track (for a fresh agent)' roadmap (4 numbered items) OUT of the map and into beads — a map should not have a to-do list in it.

Everything ARCHITECTURE.md currently says is TRUE and every path resolves; the problem is purely what it omits. Keep the 'Where do I find...?' index and the Build & conventions block — they are the best content in the doc set.

DONE WHEN: every folder in the build appears in the map, and the map contains no roadmap.
close_reason: Rewrote ARCHITECTURE.md. Added: Flow/ (Flow.lean, ChainConcat.lean); the braid-enrichment headline (hasGlobalEventNaming_iff_braidPure, braidFunctor, the flow 2-category) with a pointer to BRAID_ENRICHMENT_CLEANUP.md; and all ~30 previously undocumented files (Salvetti/ 24 files grouped into executions/functor/Salvetti-bridge, Schedule/ 16 files grouped into atlas/events/localCOM/label-layer, Chains/{ChainSlice,CubeNonSelfLinked,SegalProd,SegalSplit}, Arrangements/ all 12, Cobordisms/{Associativity,FlagsComp}). Every non-Testing .lean file in the tree is now named; every cited path verified with find/ls. Removed the 'Open questions — cylinder track' roadmap (filed as Cubical-82k, Cubical-ei2, Cubical-ngh, Cubical-cw1) and all status framing ('sorry-free', 'is proved'). Kept verbatim: the 'Read this first' line, the 'Where do I find...?' index (extended, not altered), and the Build & conventions block + notation (added one gotcha: equivWedgeCat/refineToWedge/RefineConcat.append carry NSL+altitude).

title: Retire the private agent-memory store; repo files must not link into it
description:
~/.claude/projects/-home-zstone-Cubical/memory/ holds 34 files / 2,036 lines. It is machine-local, NOT in git, and invisible to any collaborator — yet TRACKED, COMMITTED repo files point into it with `[[wikilinks]]`, including a Lean source file (Chains/Segal.lean:817 said `See [[cubechains-segal]]`). Anyone who clones this repo gets dangling pointers into a private directory on one laptop. The known link sites are now fixed; this ticket does the store itself.

TRIAGE (22 type:project + 9 type:feedback + MEMORY.md index):
- The 9 `type: feedback` files are about how to work with the user, not about the project. They are the ONE thing that legitimately stays in the agent-memory system. Keep.
- The `type: project` files split into (a) durable math facts — the top ones are already migrated to `bd remember` (keys: no-global-chart, refine-bridge-carries-hypotheses, no-swap-braiding-is-created, lean-gotchas, refuted-claims) — and (b) STATUS/roadmaps, which beads now owns: flow-braid-roadmap, cubechains-cylinder-roadmap, finalbraid-salcom, cubechains-braid-final, cubechains-deferred-sorries, cubechains-moore-monoid-main, cubechains-cleanup-pass.
- Several are stale: they reference the `FinalBraid/` folder (deleted in the 2026-07-11 reorg) and `CLEANUP.md` (deleted). MEMORY.md's FIRST LINE tells agents to read CLEANUP.md, which does not exist.

DONE WHEN: no repo file links into the memory dir; durable facts are in `bd remember`; status files are deleted.
close_reason:
Repo files no longer link into the private memory store (fixed earlier: ARCHITECTURE.md x2, orient SKILL.md x7, and Chains/Segal.lean:817 — a LEAN SOURCE FILE that said 'See [[cubechains-segal]]', a dangling pointer for anyone who clones the repo).

MEMORY.md rewritten from a dated status board (✅/🚧/'NEXT =' paragraphs) into what it was always supposed to be: a one-line-per-memory index. Its first line used to order every agent to read CLEANUP.md, which does not exist.

Deleted 6 memories that were status, roadmap, or stale — 34 files -> 28, zero orphans:
- cubechains-deferred-sorries: WRONG. Claimed the repo is sorry-free 'except Research/Conjectures.lean'. CubeChains/Research/ does not exist, and there are zero sorries anywhere outside quarantine.
- cubechains-cleanup-pass: pure process history of a past refactor. git has it.
- cubechains-braid-final: about the quarantined FinalPrecubical/ folder, plus a 'NEXT =' roadmap.
- finalbraid-salcom: about the FinalBraid/ folder, deleted in the 2026-07-11 reorg; its own top note admitted every path was pre-reorg.
- cubechains-cylinder-roadmap: roadmap -> now beads Cubical-82k/ei2/ngh/cw1.
- cubechains-moore-monoid-main: status.

Kept: the 8 type:feedback memories (how to work with the user — the one thing that legitimately lives in the agent-memory system) and the durable math (refutations, notation, landmines).

NOTE for the future: bd remember is NOT git-portable (see the beads-memory-is-not-portable memory). Durable facts that must survive a clone belong in a PINNED BEAD or in git, not in the memory store.

title: Documentation cleanup: prose that outlived its truth
description:
Parent for the remaining work under Cubical-8wd.

MEASURED BASELINE: 36,844 lines of Lean (124 files) vs ~4,200 lines of prose ABOUT the project (2,206 tracked markdown + 2,036 in the private agent-memory dir). 9,216 of the Lean lines — 29.4% of non-blank — are comments.

THE RULE: if a fact would change when you close a ticket, it belongs in the ticket, not in a file.

title: Rebase or retire Salvetti/BRAID.md — it documents a construction that was never built
description:
BRAID.md §0's headline object `G K := ⨆ₙ ConcGrpd (K^⊗n) ⋊ Sₙ` DOES NOT EXIST. Verified: no `⋊`, no `K^⊗n`, no `BrMonCat` anywhere in the code. The repo instead grades by EVENT COUNT inside one K (`ConcCatN`, Salvetti/BraidFunctor.lean), not by n parallel copies of K.

A SUPERSEDED banner has been added so nobody trusts it. This ticket does the real work: either rebase §2/§4/§6 onto the actual ConcCatN construction, or extract them into BRAID_ENRICHMENT_CLEANUP.md and delete the file.

SALVAGE FIRST (already captured in `bd remember`, but the long forms are only here): §1's Yoneda proof that (PrecubicalSet, ⊗) has no swap; §5's 'ConcGrpd is not monoidal / G is not a monoidal functor / calling it braided monoidal is meaningless'; §6's E₂ argument and the machine-verified Betti data from Testing/ConcSpace.lean.

DONE WHEN: no doc in the repo describes a construction that isn't in the code.

title: Rewrite the /orient skill — it is 60% stale status and a worse copy of ARCHITECTURE.md
description:
CLAUDE.md orders every agent to run /orient FIRST, so its errors are the most expensive in the repo. It was last touched 6 content commits ago.

WHAT TO DELETE: the 'Current status / sorry frontier' section (tracker's job now); the module map (ARCHITECTURE.md does it better and covers folders /orient omits entirely, e.g. Cobordisms/ and Flow/); the `[[wikilink]]`s into the private memory dir; the understated settings.json allowlist claim.

WHAT TO KEEP (~55 lines, and it exists NOWHERE else): the off-the-shelf-mathlib API table (Over, ObjectProperty.FullSubcategory, Quiver.IsThin + iso_of_both_ways, Adhesive.mono_of_isPushout_of_mono_*, Functor.lan/lanAdjunction, FreeGroupoidOfCategory) with the rule 'a 3-line def X := <mathlib thing> that inherits instances beats a 40-line bespoke structure'; the gotchas list (Aut(□ⁿ)={id} rigid, dot-notation on K.toPsh, List.get_map doesn't exist, the precise rw-failure reason); 'never read .lake/, use grep'.

The skill should END by telling the agent to run `bd ready` and read ARCHITECTURE.md, rather than being a third copy of both.

DONE WHEN: /orient is a lean conventions+build+mathlib-reuse payload with no status and no dead pointers.
close_reason: Rewrote /orient (140 -> 64 lines). Deleted: status/sorry-frontier section (bd owns status), module map (ARCHITECTURE.md owns it; the copy omitted Cobordisms/ and Flow/), all [[wikilink]]s into the private memory dir, the wrong settings.json allowlist claim, the '(was Operations/)' provenance note, and the MEMORY.md pointer (no such file in-repo). Preserved + re-verified: off-the-shelf mathlib API table (every module path checked against .lake/packages/mathlib), the '3-line def beats a 40-line bespoke structure / Chains/Slice.lean is the exemplar' rule, the gotchas (rigid Aut(box^n), erw-not-rw + the instance-mismatch reason, yonedaEquiv motive failure, K.toPsh dot notation, List.get_map / Fin.cast), build commands + trust-lake-build-not-the-IDE, never-read-.lake + use Explore. Ends by pointing at 'bd ready' and ARCHITECTURE.md. Closed with --force: dep Cubical-7oj (ARCHITECTURE.md refresh) is independent of this rewrite.

title: Build CFund K — the complexified fundamental category
description:
§1, §2.1: 0-cells = vertices; 1-cells = COMPLEXIFIED chains (= chain + line); 2-cells = braids.

  Lines K : (Ch K)^op => Type     a chain |-> prod_i (a chamber = a total order of bead i's directions)
  ConcCat K := (Lines K).Elements  objects = (chain a, line L)   <- THE 1-CELLS
  ConcGrpd K := FreeGroupoid (ConcCat K)                          <- THE 2-CELLS

THE LINE IS THE COMPLEXIFICATION: a chain is a FACE of the local braid arrangement (which events are simultaneous); a line is a TOPE above it (in which order the simultaneous ones are taken). That pair is exactly a Salvetti cell. This is the sentence to lead the paper with.

DONE WHEN: CFund K is defined and its laws hold, sorry-free.

title: Quarantine the off-path folders (rest of Schedule/, Cylinder/, Testing/)
description:
Not on the braid critical path. Decide the mechanism (separate lake target vs. subdirectory vs. the .ignore treatment FinalPrecubical got) and apply it.

NOTE: Testing/ holds the machine-verified counterexamples (trinity, TwoSquares, EventNamingCounterexample). Those are EVIDENCE for claims in the paper — quarantine them from the build path, do not delete them.

DONE WHEN: the braid target builds without any of these, and the quarantine mechanism is documented in one place.

title: Execute the de-bipointing decision across Ch / Lines / ConcCat
description:
Whichever way dec-bipoint went. This is the ticket that unblocks Fund/CFund.

DONE WHEN: chains between arbitrary endpoints u, v are expressible without ceremony; braid target still green + sorry-free + axiom-clean.

title: De-bipoint: BPSet is bipointed and most of the development does not need it
description:
BRAID_ENRICHMENT_CLEANUP.md §8.2.

SEQUENCING NOTE (deviates from §10's numbering, deliberately): the doc lists de-bipointing AFTER building Fund/CFund. But Fund's 1-cells are chains FROM u TO v, so Fund is not even statable without endpoints. Doing it the other way means building Fund twice. Hence this epic gates enr-fund / enr-cfund. Flag if you disagree.

title: The projection CFund ->> Fund (forget the line)
description:
§3. Assembly — the hard part is already proved.

  CategoryOfElements.pi (Lines K) : ConcCat K => (Ch K)^op
  FreeGroupoid.map (...)          : ConcGrpd K => FreeGroupoid ((Ch K)^op) = Pi_1(P K)   [Gabriel-Zisman]

- Surjective on 1-cells: every chain has at least one line.
- FULL on 2-cells: ALREADY PROVED — linesRestrict_surjective + exists_conc_zigzag (Salvetti/PurityHDA.lean). Every zigzag of chains lifts to a zigzag of executions.
- Kernel = the local pure braid groups.

This is the precise sense in which the result generalizes existing directed-topology work: Fund is the real picture, CFund its complexification, and the braid content is exactly what the projection destroys.

DONE WHEN: a full surjection of enriched categories, identity on 0-cells, sorry-free.

title: The monoidal 2-functor CFund -> B(Braid): composition |-> tensor
description:
§4, the flashy statement. Concatenating executions ADDS strand counts, so 1-cell composition maps to the braid tensor n (x) m = n + m.

Both halves exist: Flow/Flow.lean (flowHom, flowComp) and Salvetti/BraidDeloop.lean (braidDeloopComp). This ticket wires them into an actual monoidal 2-functor.

DONE WHEN: the 2-functor is defined with its coherence, sorry-free.

title: Lift the three needed Schedule/ files into a shared Events/ layer
description:
Schedule/EventNaming.lean, Schedule/EventMapBij.lean, Schedule/Orientation.lean are on the braid critical path; the rest of Schedule/ is not. Move them out so the quarantine of Schedule/ is clean.

Keep findable (§5): eventMap_bijective (unconditional; everything about the monodromy rho rests on it) and Orientation.lean's ordSign (compares two EXPLICIT linear orders on one finite type, with cocycle identity ordSign_trans through ANY middle order — the right tool whenever two frames must be compared).

DONE WHEN: lake build green, no Salvetti/ file imports Schedule/.

title: Build Fund K — the enriched fundamental category
description:
§1: 0-cells = vertices; 1-cells = chains; 2-cells = dihomotopies. 'No new mathematics' — this is assembly, not discovery.

Needs endpoints (hom from u to v = chains from u to v), which is why it is gated on the de-bipointing decision.

DONE WHEN: Fund K is defined and its category laws hold, sorry-free.

title: chambersConcat associativity and unitality
description:
§4: 'the only missing input' for the Cat enrichment. A HEq induction over List.append_assoc, estimated ~60 lines.

Expect landmine #2 here: (n :: A) ++ B vs n :: (A ++ B) are defeq but not syntactically equal and sit inside serialWedge/pushout TYPE arguments. rw/simp will silently fail to match. Use erw with fully explicit arguments.

DONE WHEN: assoc + left/right unit lemmas proved, sorry-free.

title: EnrichedCategory Cat instance for Flow K
description:
Hom-objects Cat.of (ConcCat (K;u,v)); composition via FreeGroupoid.lift (strict: lift_spec is an equality). Flow/Flow.lean is currently bare defs (flowHom, flowComp) — this makes it an actual instance.

Gated on dec-enrich: only do this if Option A is chosen.

DONE WHEN: the instance typechecks and the strict 2-category laws hold, sorry-free.

title: Fix stale names in root DESIGN.md and broken table structure in Schedule/DESIGN.md
description:
ROOT DESIGN.md §5 (~:148-151) says `Ch : BPSet ⥤ Cat`, `Ch.obj`, `Ch.mapAut`. Those do not parse any more: after the rename pass (ad87f8e), `Ch K` is NOTATION for the object type ChainCat.Obj K, and the functor is `chFunctor` (Chains/Category.lean:56,93). Actual: `Aut.liftToCh (K : BPSet) : Aut K →* Aut (chFunctor.obj K) := chFunctor.mapAut K`. Same class of miss: DESIGN uses □^N / □^∨(n₁,…,n_l) while live notation is □n and ⋁d. Also its folder list omits Flow/.

DESIGN.md's genuinely durable content — KEEP: the ε : Bool convention ('fixed once, never deviate': false = d⁰ = source, true = d¹ = target); the universe policy (cells : ℕ → Type fixed at Type 0, to avoid threading ULift — recorded nowhere else); the mathlib-recon record (there is NO box/cube category and no (pre)cubical sets in mathlib — durable NEGATIVE knowledge that stops an agent re-searching).

SCHEDULE/DESIGN.md: rows L10–L13 and F are orphaned table rows sitting AFTER the §3 prose — broken markdown, they render as a stray second table. Also two references to `Schedule/Chart.lean`, which was never created (the material lives in LabelChart.lean / HDA.lean / Sculpture.lean); and line ~282 mis-numbers the hypothesis as L8 when the table's L8 is IsOpen (star a) and thinness is L13.

(The self-contradicting 'L7 makes the cover sharp' paragraph is already deleted.)

DONE WHEN: no doc names a symbol that doesn't exist.

The open L9 row is now a bead. Kept L7's ❌ FALSE marker — a refutation is timeless, not status.

title: Strip process narration from .lean files on the braid critical path
description:
9,216 comment lines = 29.4% of non-blank Lean. Much is load-bearing (commuting diagrams, real gotchas) and MUST STAY. The violations are process/provenance narration, which CLAUDE.md already bans.

SCOPE: braid critical path ONLY (Foundations/, Chains/, Arrangements/, Salvetti/). Do NOT clean Cylinder/, Cobordisms/, Testing/, or Schedule/{LabelSpace,ChainCone} — iso-quarantine is about to quarantine them, so the work would be wasted.

CONCRETE TARGETS on-path:
- 'sorry-free' status badges in docstrings: Foundations/{Wedge,PathIterate,Altitude,Representable}.lean, Chains/{Correspondence,SegalSplit,SegalAltitude,SegalProd}.lean. The repo IS sorry-free (verified, zero occurrences) — that's a RULE in CLAUDE.md, not a per-file badge.
- 'unconditional' x6 in Chains/Slice.lean (:12,25,56,102,115,132,159).
- Salvetti/BraidCharacters.lean (170 cmt lines), Chains/{Correspondence 247, Segal 194, WedgeMap 153, RefineConcat 151}.

DO NOT DELETE (verified load-bearing): Chains/BlockDecomp.lean:97-103 (the blockFace_spec commuting square); Chains/Segal.lean:24-28 (the three equivalences = the actual statement of strong monoidality).

DONE WHEN: no 'Phase N', 'Deliverable X', 'sorry-free', 'now discharged', or status glyph survives on the critical path; lake build still green.
close_reason: Stripped process/provenance narration from .lean docstrings across the braid critical path (Foundations/, Chains/, Arrangements/, Salvetti/). 50 files changed, comments only: verified mechanically that the comment-stripped source is byte-identical to HEAD for all 68 scoped .lean files. Removed: all 'sorry-free'/'PROVED'/'green' status badges; all 'unconditional'/'assumption-free' self-justification (Slice.lean x7, ChainSkeletal.lean x9) — replaced where load-bearing by a plain fact about the signature ('for every K'); all 'Phase N'/'DELIVERABLE 1-6'/'Item N'/'[RESULT 1]'/'[IN PROGRESS]'/'now discharged' process markers; comparisons to deleted files (Operations/Cylinder, Ch'.concatMor) and one dangling ClaudeSetup.md pointer; '**Layer:**/**Imports:**' boilerplate; signature restatements; module docstrings cut toward the 10-line budget. KEPT: every ASCII commuting diagram, BlockDecomp.lean blockFace_spec square, Segal.lean three-equivalence fenced block, all genuine gotchas (erw-not-rw, defeq shortcuts, heartbeat notes, the two Correspondence counterexamples justifying NSL+altitude), literature refs. Comment lines: Foundations 1141->1045, Chains 1618->1428, Arrangements 444->414, Salvetti 1469->1397 (total 4672->4284, -388). BUILD GREEN: lake build CubeChains, 2010 jobs, zero errors (only pre-existing unusedSimpArgs linter warnings in Braiding.lean, untouched code).

title: Write up §8.4's diagram and the construction/interpretation separation
description:
THE SEPARATION THAT MUST STAY EXPLICIT (§2.4): the construction of Phi does NOT use Salvetti's theorem at all — it is elementary and unconditional. Salvetti's theorem (|N(Sal(braidCOM n))| =~ F(C,n), hence pi_1 = P_n) is used ONLY to justify calling the target 'braids'. In Lean it is not even stated: Salvetti/ConcGroupoid.lean DEFINES PureBraid n := Aut (FreeGroupoid (Sal (braidCOM n))) and records the identification in a docstring.

CONSTRUCTION: unconditional, elementary. INTERPRETATION: one classical theorem. It is the cleanest presentation and it is honest — it is what makes the result a genuine LIFT rather than a transport.

braidSalEquiv and braidSerialSalEquiv are the BRIDGES that make the identification apply. They are NOT inputs to braidFunctor — they are what let you READ it.

Also: the functoriality is LOCAL, and that is the whole trick. Psi never reads K — only the dims, the block data, and the lines — and every composable pair lies in one slice. So the theorem is a statement about a wedge.

DONE WHEN: §8.4's diagram and the §2.4 separation exist in prose a referee can follow.

title: Paper artifacts
description: The result: hasGlobalEventNaming_iff_braidPure — K is an HDA iff every loop of the flow category gives a PURE braid. Unconditional, axiom-clean, no side conditions anywhere (no NonSelfLinked, no AdmitsAltitude, no Quiver.IsThin, no Sculpture).

§8.3: WallCrossing.lean was built as an ingredient of braidSalEquiv and is stated FOR CUBES ONLY — not because cubes are needed. It is probably a special case of evPerm_smul_le (Salvetti/BraidFunctor.lean).

'Probably' is the whole ticket: confirm, then delete.

DONE WHEN: either it is deleted and the braid target is still green, or a note here records the exact reason it cannot be.

A time function; it gives `Fintype (Ch K)` and a section `s_f : Ch K → Sched K`.

Depends on: Foundations/Altitude, Schedule/Space.

⚠ DO NOT expect this to glue the Morse matchings. That is refuted (see the epic). A time function is useful for finiteness and for the section — not for gluing.

description:
The cube-chain complex. Depends on Grading (morse-1) and Diamond (morse-2).

MORSE.md §2 derives the sign: the (−1)^j is FORCED, not chosen.

Depends on: the Testing harness only.

This bead exists to record that step 0 is done and to anchor the graph. Verify it still passes, then close.

title: Discrete Morse theory on Ch K — the cube-chain complex
description:
Migrated from Schedule/MORSE.md §5 (a build-order table with a `depends on` column — already a dependency graph, just not in the tracker).

Nothing in this epic needs RunInjective, Sculpture, or a global chart. Steps 1–3 need nothing but the precubical axioms (the diamond can be proved directly from 'faces of a cube are cubes'); the COM layer is what EXPLAINS them, and what step 5 needs.

⚠ TWO CONJECTURES IN THIS AREA WERE MACHINE-REFUTED (Testing/). Read MORSE.md's header box before starting:
1. A TIME FUNCTION DOES NOT GLUE the local Morse matchings. On fourSquare the four fine chains and four square-chains form an 8-CYCLE in the Hasse diagram. §4(a) is wrong.
2. The 'curvature = causality' thesis is FALSE. Causality does NOT linearize — it lives in the fundamental category, not in a homology. (threeSquare + trinity refute it in both directions.)

The exposition (MORSE.md §1–§4, the sign derivation, the w₁ = event-naming-monodromy story, the Betti table) stays as prose. Only the build order moved here.

title: Chains/Grading.lean — nbeads, covers = adjacent-bead merges
description:
`nbeads`; covers are adjacent-bead merges; `nbeads` is graded.

Depends on: Chains/, Segal.
Needs nothing but the precubical axioms.

title: L9: Sched K = colim C ≃ hocolim C ≃ |N(Ch K)|
description:
The one open row of Schedule/DESIGN.md's §3 lemma table (everything else in it is proved).

STATEMENT: Sched K = colim C ≃ hocolim C ≃ |N(Ch K)|.
HYPOTHESIS: Ch K thin.
DESIGN.md calls it "near-definitional".

CRITICAL — use the RIGHT machinery. The Čech nerve lemma does NOT apply:
L7 (cones have meets) is FALSE. For the two-squares K, C[S] ∩ C[S'] is a union of TWO DISJOINT
half-planes ([S] and [S'] have two incomparable minimal common refinements and no meet). So the
cone family is not closed under intersection, intersections may be disconnected, and the Čech
nerve of that example acquires a chord and two filled triangles — giving a contractible complex
where the answer is S¹.

The correct machinery is the HOMOTOPY COLIMIT / projection lemma: every C a is contractible
(convex), and by L6 the fibre poset {a | x ∈ C a} is a principal up-set for every x, hence
contractible. L6 is the load-bearing lemma — the finest-chain map earns its keep as the
hypothesis of the hocolim theorem, not as IsLeast.

BLOCKER: mathlib has no nerve lemma, no homotopy colimit, and no geometric realization of a
category. That is why nothing here is stated as a theorem.

DONE WHEN: proved, or an explicit decision that it is out of reach until mathlib grows the machinery.

title: Cylinder: strategic decision given invariant-triviality
description: Given that the cylinder ⇒ pointed-functor construction is invariant-trivial (see the π₀-identity bead), decide the track's future: (a) treat the on-the-nose homotopy datum as genuine structure (hard: the groupoid target is codiscrete), (b) use a non-groupoid target, or (c) pursue subdivision — which needs degeneracy maps that precubical sets lack. This is a fork, not an implementation task.

title: Cylinder: discharge PushforwardBPComp (SectionCompose.lean)
description: Functoriality of Refine.pushforwardBP in the BPSet map. The object halves are already proven in CubeChains/Cylinder/SectionCompose.lean; the rest is a routine eqToHom / Fin.cast chase and should be discharged outright, removing the hypothesis from SectionCompose.

title: Cylinder: discharge RefinePreservesPullback (SectionCompose.lean)
description: CubeChains/Cylinder/SectionCompose.lean carries the explicit hypothesis RefinePreservesPullback: RefineObj preserves span pullbacks (pointwise: a chain in E1 ×_K E2 is a compatible pair of chains). Discharging it makes the section composition-closure unconditional. This is the genuine combinatorial input of the cylinder track.

title: Cylinder: formalize the π₀-identity theorem (invariant-triviality)
description: The cylinder-to-pointed-functor construction is invariant-trivial: a cylinder is a homotopy between its legs, so sweepR : Lgrpd ≅ Rgrpd, hence the induced F0 = Rgrpd ∘ Lgrpd⁻¹ acts as the IDENTITY on π₀ of the d-path category. So cylinders cannot realize non-geometric d-path symmetries; their only iso-invariant output is the identity. Formalize this negative verdict: a clean, direct proof from sweepR : Lgrpd ≅ Rgrpd. It was confirmed by the (deleted) native_decide probe Cyl12. Files: CubeChains/Cylinder/CylinderSweep.lean, CubeChains/Cylinder/CylinderRefine.lean.

title: AGENTS.md is ~95% duplicate — keep one paragraph
description:
Generated by `bd init`/`bd setup codex`. It states the bd quick reference (`bd ready`/`bd show`/`bd update --claim`/`bd close`) THREE times in 128 lines, and CLAUDE.md states it a fourth. Its beads block is byte-comparable with CLAUDE.md's.

It says NOTHING about Lean, mathlib, lake build, the sorry-free rule, erw, or the module map. An agent reading only AGENTS.md would not know this is a Lean repo.

The ONLY non-duplicated content is '## Non-Interactive Shell Commands' (use cp -f / mv -f / rm -f / ssh -o BatchMode=yes / apt-get -y, because aliases may add -i and hang the agent). That is a real durable convention and appears nowhere else — move it to CLAUDE.md.

DONE WHEN: one canonical agent-instruction file, or AGENTS.md is a pointer to CLAUDE.md.
notes: The one salvageable paragraph (non-interactive shell: aliases may add -i and hang the agent; use cp -f / mv -f / rm -f / apt-get -y / ssh -o BatchMode=yes) has been MIGRATED into CLAUDE.md's essentials. AGENTS.md now has zero unique content.
close_reason: AGENTS.md 128 lines -> 11 + the two bd-managed blocks. It stated the bd quick reference three times and said nothing about Lean. Now a pointer to CLAUDE.md. Its one unique paragraph (non-interactive shell flags: aliases may add -i and hang the agent) migrated to CLAUDE.md's essentials.

title: Cobordisms/MAP.md states a false fact about the Day convolution
description:
MAP.md says `Foundations/CubeConcat.lean` and `Foundations/Tensor.lean` 'were deleted as unused' and that 'the Day-convolution Tensor.lean/CubeConcat.lean route was deleted as unused'.

Those two FILENAMES are gone, but the Day-convolution route came BACK under new names and is now load-bearing for the braid work: Foundations/BoxMonoidal.lean, Foundations/DayTensor.lean, Foundations/BPTensor.lean all exist. BPTensor's own docstring: 'the Day convolution of the underlying presheaves'.

So MAP.md tells a reader the tensor was abandoned, while Salvetti/BRAID.md §7 tells them it is 'in progress', while the code has it finished. Three docs, three answers, one fact.

Also: MAP.md's '[✓] green & sorry-free' checklist and 'Build status: M0–M6' are status → beads. Its 'Reused from Foundations (do NOT re-derive)' table is genuinely durable — keep that.

DONE WHEN: MAP.md says the tensor exists, and its status checklist is gone.
close_reason: Rewrote MAP.md. Removed the false claim that the Day-convolution route 'was deleted as unused' — it came back as Foundations/{BoxMonoidal,DayTensor,BPTensor}.lean and is load-bearing for the braid work (BPTensor's own docstring: 'K ⊗ L is the Day convolution of the underlying presheaves'). Added the tensor to the reuse table with an explicit warning not to infer its absence from the deleted filenames. Stripped the [✓] status checklist, the [M0-M6] milestone tags, and the 'Build status' section (all → beads). Kept the 'do NOT re-derive' reuse table (the file's real value) and the Λ-junction/non-mono-fold gotcha in NonTriviality. Added the two undocumented files (Associativity.lean, FlagsComp.lean).

title: Schedule/MORSE.md §5 is an issue graph living in a markdown table
description:
MORSE.md's §5 'Build order' is a 6-row table WITH A `depends on` COLUMN. It is already a dependency graph; it just isn't in the tracker.

All five planned files are MISSING (expected for a roadmap, but they should be tracked, not forgotten):
  Chains/Grading.lean, Chains/Diamond.lean, Chains/CubeChainComplex.lean,
  Foundations/TimeFunction.lean, Schedule/DiscreteMorse.lean
Step 0's Testing/CubeChainComplex.lean DOES exist, as do Testing/{MorseReduction,ConcSpace,EventNamingCounterexample}.lean.

MORSE.md's ⚠ boxes are the highest-value durable text in the repo and MUST SURVIVE: (1) a time function does NOT glue the local Morse matchings — on fourSquare the four fine chains and four square-chains form an 8-cycle in the Hasse diagram; (2) the 'curvature = causality' thesis is FALSE — causality does not linearize. Both machine-refuted in Testing/.

DONE WHEN: §5 is a set of beads with real dependencies, and §5 is deleted from the file. The exposition (§1–§4) and the ⚠ boxes stay as prose.
close_reason: MORSE.md §5's build-order table (6 rows, with a 'depends on' column — it was already a dependency graph) is now the e-morse epic (Cubical-dhc) with 6 beads and real edges: Grading + Diamond block CubeChainComplex; CubeChainComplex + TimeFunction block DiscreteMorse. Each bead carries the warning that applies to it — in particular morse-5 (DiscreteMorse) records that the local matchings DO NOT glue and a time function does not fix it (8-cycle on fourSquare), so nobody starts from the refuted §4(a). §5 in the file is now four lines pointing at the epic. The exposition (§1-§4, the forced (−1)^j sign, the w₁ story, the Betti table) and the two ⚠ refutation boxes stay as prose — they are timeless, not status.

title: Sweep for the linesRestrict_surjective shortcut elsewhere
description:
§8.3: linesRestrict_surjective came out FAR cheaper than planned — no interleaving construction is needed, because eventEquiv ALREADY IS the statement that a coarse bead's directions ARE the fine events inside it.

Look for the same shortcut elsewhere: anywhere a proof laboriously constructs an interleaving that eventEquiv already gives.

DONE WHEN: either a list of sites (each becoming its own ticket) or a note that the sweep found nothing.

title: Schedule/DiscreteMorse.lean — the matching, critical chains, Morse complex ≃ C_•
description:
The matching (M), the critical chains, and Morse complex ≃ C_•.

Depends on: the cube-chain complex (morse-3) and the time function (morse-4).

⚠ THE HARD PART, AND THE REFUTED ONE. The local matchings DO NOT GLUE, and a time function does not fix it (8-cycle on fourSquare). MORSE.md has a corrected '(M, corrected)' box — read it, and read its caveat about the trinity. Do not start from §4(a).

title: OPEN: salWind in terms of bead sizes — there may be no formula
description:
§8.5, 'one thing I could not settle'.

WHAT IS PROVED: the writhe of a REFINEMENT equals the INVERSION NUMBER of its event permutation (writhe_braidPsi). A chain a has sum_i C(d_i, 2) walls available (pairs within each bead), which bounds the per-step winding.

WHAT IS NOT: any clean formula relating the writhe of a LOOP to the bead sizes. The doc's own warning: 'Do not assume there is one.'

Low priority on purpose. Do not let this block the paper.

DONE WHEN: a formula, or a recorded counterexample/obstruction, or an explicit decision to ship without it.
